title: Croque Madame

overview.description: A classic French toasted ham and cheese sandwich topped with creamy béchamel, melted Gruyère and a fried egg.

method: Melt the butter in a small saucepan.
Whisk in the flour and cook for 1 minute.
Gradually whisk in the milk until smooth.
Season with salt, pepper and a pinch of nutmeg.
Butter both sides of the bread before frying or air frying to toast
Place the ham and half of the Gruyère between two slices of bread.
Spread the béchamel over the tops of the sandwiches.
Bake under a hot grill until golden and bubbling.
Meanwhile fry the eggs until the whites are set and the yolks remain runny.
Place one egg on top of each sandwich and serve immediately.

learning.did-you-know: The fried egg turns a Croque Monsieur into a Croque Madame, supposedly because the egg resembles a lady's hat.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How a simple béchamel transforms a toasted sandwich into a classic French café dish.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A comforting weekend breakfast or light lunch that introduces another classic French technique using familiar ingredients.
chapman-tips: Gruyère gives the traditional flavour, but mature Cheddar makes an excellent substitute.
Don't make the béchamel too thick—it should spread easily.
Toast the bread lightly first so it stays crisp underneath.
A runny yolk is part of what makes this dish special.
